metadata.description: Name of the existing APIM instance in this resource group
Display name for the new API
Rate limit: max calls per minute per IP
Backend timeout in seconds (image generation can be slow)

variables.$fxv#0:
<!--
  API-level shared policy for openai-images-v1.
  Applied to ALL operations before individual operation policies.
  
  Caller auth: APIM subscription key (header: api-key)
  Backend auth: APIM managed identity → Azure AD token → Azure OpenAI
  
  Handles: request-id, rate-limit, managed identity auth, timeout, error normalization.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />

    <!-- ── 1. Generate / propagate request_id ── -->
    <set-variable name="requestId" value="@{
      var clientId = context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("X-Request-Id", "");
      return string.IsNullOrEmpty(clientId) ? context.RequestId.ToString() : clientId;
    }" />
    <set-header name="X-Request-Id" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["requestId"])</value>
    </set-header>

    <!-- ── 2. Rate limiting per caller IP ── -->
    <rate-limit-by-key calls="{{openai-images-rate-limit}}" renewal-period="60" counter-key="@(context.Request.IpAddress)" />

    <!-- ── 3. Strip any client auth headers, set backend URL ── -->
    <set-header name="Authorization" exists-action="delete" />
    <set-header name="api-key" exists-action="delete" />
    <set-backend-service base-url="{{openai-images-foundry-endpoint}}" />

    <!-- ── 4. Authenticate to Azure OpenAI via managed identity ── -->
    <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com" />

  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <forward-request timeout="{{openai-images-timeout}}" />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <!-- Inject request_id header into every response -->
    <set-header name="X-Request-Id" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["requestId"])</value>
    </set-header>
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
    <!-- Unified error normalization -->
    <set-variable name="errorCode" value="@{
      var status = context.Response != null ? context.Response.StatusCode : 500;
      if (context.LastError != null && context.LastError.Reason == "OperationTimedOut") {
        return "UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT";
      }
      switch (status) {
        case 400: return "INVALID_REQUEST";
        case 401: return "UNAUTHORIZED";
        case 403: return "FORBIDDEN";
        case 404: return "NOT_FOUND";
        case 429: return "RATE_LIMITED";
        default: return status >= 500 ? "UPSTREAM_ERROR" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
      }
    }" />
    <set-variable name="errorStatus" value="@{
      var code = (string)context.Variables["errorCode"];
      switch (code) {
        case "INVALID_REQUEST": return 400;
        case "UNAUTHORIZED": return 401;
        case "FORBIDDEN": return 403;
        case "NOT_FOUND": return 404;
        case "RATE_LIMITED": return 429;
        case "UPSTREAM_TIMEOUT": return 504;
        case "UPSTREAM_ERROR": return 502;
        default: return 500;
      }
    }" />
    <set-variable name="errorMessage" value="@{
      if (context.Response != null) {
        try {
          var body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);
          var err = body?["error"];
          if (err != null) {
            var innerErr = err["innererror"];
            if (innerErr != null) {
              var filterCode = innerErr["code"]?.ToString();
              if (filterCode == "ResponsibleAIPolicyViolation") {
                return "Content filtered by safety policy.";
              }
            }
            var msg = err["message"]?.ToString();
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(msg)) {
              return msg;
            }
          }
        } catch { }
      }
      if (context.LastError != null) {
        return context.LastError.Message;
      }
      return "An unexpected error occurred.";
    }" />
    <set-variable name="finalErrorCode" value="@{
      var msg = (string)context.Variables["errorMessage"];
      if (msg.Contains("Content filtered") || msg.Contains("ResponsibleAIPolicyViolation") || msg.Contains("safety policy")) {
        return "CONTENT_FILTERED";
      }
      return (string)context.Variables["errorCode"];
    }" />

    <return-response>
      <set-status code="@((int)context.Variables["errorStatus"])" reason="Error" />
      <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="X-Request-Id" exists-action="override">
        <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId") ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"] : context.RequestId.ToString())</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        var requestId = context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId") ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"] : context.RequestId.ToString();
        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("success", false),
          new JProperty("request_id", requestId),
          new JProperty("error", new JObject(
            new JProperty("code", (string)context.Variables["finalErrorCode"]),
            new JProperty("message", (string)context.Variables["errorMessage"])
          ))
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </return-response>
  </on-error>
</policies>
variables.$fxv#1:
<!--
  Operation policy: POST /images/generations
  Routes JSON body to Foundry image generation endpoint.
  Normalizes successful response to the project's standard envelope.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />
    <set-variable name="backendUrl" value="@{
      var deployment = "{{openai-images-deployment-name}}";
      var apiVer = "{{openai-images-api-version}}";
      return String.Format("/openai/deployments/{0}/images/generations?api-version={1}", deployment, apiVer);
    }" />
    <rewrite-uri template="@((string)context.Variables["backendUrl"])" copy-unmatched-params="false" />
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <base />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode == 200)">
        <set-body>@{
          var requestId = context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId")
            ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"]
            : context.RequestId.ToString();
          var body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);

          var created = body?["created"] != null
            ? body["created"].Value<long>()
            : DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds();

          var images = new JArray();
          var data = body?["data"] as JArray;
          if (data != null) {
            foreach (var item in data) {
              var imageObj = new JObject(
                new JProperty("b64_json", item["b64_json"]?.ToString() ?? ""),
                new JProperty("mime_type", "image/png")
              );
              if (item["revised_prompt"] != null) {
                imageObj.Add(new JProperty("revised_prompt", item["revised_prompt"].ToString()));
              }
              images.Add(imageObj);
            }
          }

          return new JObject(
            new JProperty("success", true),
            new JProperty("request_id", requestId),
            new JProperty("provider", "azure-foundry"),
            new JProperty("mode", "text_to_image"),
            new JProperty("created", created),
            new JProperty("images", images)
          ).ToString();
        }</set-body>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>

<!--
  Operation policy: POST /images/edits
  Forwards multipart/form-data to Foundry image edit endpoint.
  Normalizes successful response to the project's standard envelope.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />
    <set-variable name="backendUrl" value="@{
      var deployment = "{{openai-images-deployment-name}}";
      var apiVer = "{{openai-images-api-version}}";
      return String.Format("/openai/deployments/{0}/images/edits?api-version={1}", deployment, apiVer);
    }" />
    <rewrite-uri template="@((string)context.Variables["backendUrl"])" copy-unmatched-params="false" />
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <base />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode == 200)">
        <set-body>@{
          var requestId = context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId")
            ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"]
            : context.RequestId.ToString();
          var body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);

          var created = body?["created"] != null
            ? body["created"].Value<long>()
            : DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds();

          var images = new JArray();
          var data = body?["data"] as JArray;
          if (data != null) {
            foreach (var item in data) {
              var imageObj = new JObject(
                new JProperty("b64_json", item["b64_json"]?.ToString() ?? ""),
                new JProperty("mime_type", "image/png")
              );
              if (item["revised_prompt"] != null) {
                imageObj.Add(new JProperty("revised_prompt", item["revised_prompt"].ToString()));
              }
              images.Add(imageObj);
            }
          }

          return new JObject(
            new JProperty("success", true),
            new JProperty("request_id", requestId),
            new JProperty("provider", "azure-foundry"),
            new JProperty("mode", "image_edit"),
            new JProperty("created", created),
            new JProperty("images", images)
          ).ToString();
        }</set-body>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>

<!--
  Operation policy: POST /images/variations
  Public path is separate from /images/edits, but internally routes
  to the same Foundry /images/edits endpoint.
  Normalizes successful response with mode=image_variation.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />
    <set-variable name="backendUrl" value="@{
      var deployment = "{{openai-images-deployment-name}}";
      var apiVer = "{{openai-images-api-version}}";
      return String.Format("/openai/deployments/{0}/images/edits?api-version={1}", deployment, apiVer);
    }" />
    <rewrite-uri template="@((string)context.Variables["backendUrl"])" copy-unmatched-params="false" />
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <base />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
    <choose>
      <when condition="@(context.Response.StatusCode == 200)">
        <set-body>@{
          var requestId = context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId")
            ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"]
            : context.RequestId.ToString();
          var body = context.Response.Body.As<JObject>(preserveContent: true);

          var created = body?["created"] != null
            ? body["created"].Value<long>()
            : DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToUnixTimeSeconds();

          var images = new JArray();
          var data = body?["data"] as JArray;
          if (data != null) {
            foreach (var item in data) {
              var imageObj = new JObject(
                new JProperty("b64_json", item["b64_json"]?.ToString() ?? ""),
                new JProperty("mime_type", "image/png")
              );
              if (item["revised_prompt"] != null) {
                imageObj.Add(new JProperty("revised_prompt", item["revised_prompt"].ToString()));
              }
              images.Add(imageObj);
            }
          }

          return new JObject(
            new JProperty("success", true),
            new JProperty("request_id", requestId),
            new JProperty("provider", "azure-foundry"),
            new JProperty("mode", "image_variation"),
            new JProperty("created", created),
            new JProperty("images", images)
          ).ToString();
        }</set-body>
      </when>
    </choose>
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>
variables.$fxv#4:
<!--
  Operation policy: GET /health
  Returns a gateway health response directly from APIM.
  Optionally probes Foundry reachability with a lightweight send-request.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />

    <!-- Probe Foundry reachability (lightweight GET to the deployment endpoint) -->
    <send-request mode="new" response-variable-name="foundryProbe" timeout="10" ignore-error="true">
      <set-url>@{
        var endpoint = "{{openai-images-foundry-endpoint}}";
        var deployment = "{{openai-images-deployment-name}}";
        var apiVer = "{{openai-images-api-version}}";
        return String.Format("{0}/openai/deployments/{1}?api-version={2}", endpoint, deployment, apiVer);
      }</set-url>
      <set-method>GET</set-method>
      <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com" />
    </send-request>

    <set-variable name="foundryReachable" value="@{
      if (context.Variables.ContainsKey("foundryProbe")) {
        var resp = (IResponse)context.Variables["foundryProbe"];
        return resp.StatusCode >= 200 && resp.StatusCode < 500;
      }
      return false;
    }" />

    <!-- Return health response directly (don't forward to backend) -->
    <return-response>
      <set-status code="200" reason="OK" />
      <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
        <value>application/json</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-header name="X-Request-Id" exists-action="override">
        <value>@(context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId") ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"] : context.RequestId.ToString())</value>
      </set-header>
      <set-body>@{
        var requestId = context.Variables.ContainsKey("requestId")
          ? (string)context.Variables["requestId"]
          : context.RequestId.ToString();
        var foundryOk = (bool)context.Variables["foundryReachable"];
        var overall = foundryOk ? "healthy" : "degraded";

        return new JObject(
          new JProperty("success", true),
          new JProperty("request_id", requestId),
          new JProperty("service", "ai-gateway"),
          new JProperty("status", overall),
          new JProperty("timestamp", DateTimeOffset.UtcNow.ToString("o")),
          new JProperty("dependencies", new JObject(
            new JProperty("apim", new JObject(
              new JProperty("status", "healthy")
            )),
            new JProperty("foundry", new JObject(
              new JProperty("status", foundryOk ? "healthy" : "unreachable"),
              new JProperty("deployment", "{{openai-images-deployment-name}}")
            ))
          ))
        ).ToString();
      }</set-body>
    </return-response>
  </inbound>

  <backend>
    <!-- No backend call — handled entirely in inbound -->
    <base />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <base />
  </outbound>

  <on-error>
    <base />
  </on-error>
</policies>

properties.title: Azure OpenAI Foundry (gpt-image-1.5)
properties.description: Azure Global Foundry endpoint for image generation
OpenAI-style image generation, editing and variation APIs backed by Azure AI Foundry gpt-image-1.5
Generate images from a text prompt using gpt-image-1.5
Edit an existing image with a text prompt
Create a variation of an existing image
Gateway health check with optional Foundry reachability probe
